titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Clarence Day Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 10533823.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday April 14, 2018 at 19:53

did.unittitle: Clarence Day collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, personal papers, and printed material documenting the career of Clarence Shepard Day. The collection provides insight into Day's work as an author, illustrator, and humorist, who often poked fun at upper class New Yorkers. The collection also documents Day's experience as an undergraduate student at Yale University in the late nineteenth-century. Correspondents include: Alfred A. Knopf and Vira Whitehouse.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired by gift and purchase from various sources, 1939-1995. For more information consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Clarence Day Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Clarence Day Collection. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization, in 2012.
This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call numbers: Za Day and Uncat Za Ms Day.

bioghist.head: Clarence Day (1874-1935)
bioghist.p: Clarence Shepard Day, Jr., American author, illustrator, and humorist, was born in New York City to Clarence Shepard and Lavinia Elizabeth Stockwell Day on November 18, 1874.

Day graduated from Yale University in 1896 with a Bachelor of Arts. After graduation Day worked as a stockbroker in his father's company. He trained with the U.S. Navy during the Spanish-American War, serving on the Nahant , which remained in the New York Harbor. Day suffered from arthritis, which forced him to leave the military and eventually to end his career as a stockbroker.

Day's illness prompted him to begin a career as a freelance writer and illustrator. He was the owner and publisher of the Yale Alumni Weekly (1905-1909), published pieces in Harper's , New Yorker , Saturday Evening Post , Yale Review , and New Republic , and completed freelance work for other authors. Day also wrote book reviews and a financial column for the Metropolitan Magazine under the pseudonym B. H. Arkwright (1915-1921).

Day's books include: This Simian World (1920), The Crow's Nest (1921), Thoughts without Words (1928), God and My Father (1932), In the Green Mountain Country (1934), Scenes from the Mesozoic (1935), Life with Father (1935), After All (a revision of The Crow's Nest , 1936), and Life with Mother (1937). Life with Father was a bestseller in 1935 and 1936 and was a Book-of-the-Month Club selection. The book was also adapted for theatre, film, and television.
Day married Katherine Briggs Dodge in 1928 with whom he had one daughter (Wendy Day). Day died at his home in New York City of pneumonia on December 28, 1935.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, personal papers, and printed material documenting the career of Clarence Shepard Day. The collection provides insight into Day's work as an author, illustrator, and humorist, who often poked fun at upper class New Yorkers. The collection also documents Day's experience as an undergraduate student at Yale University in the late nineteenth-century.

Day's creative life is represented in his correspondence with publisher Alfred A. Knopf, as well as in his illustrated letters to friends including Albert Galloway Keller, Mary Thompson, and Vira Whitehouse. Drafts for Scenes from the Mesozoic reflect Day's work as an author and humorist. The collection does not include extensive documents pertaining to Day's most well-known publication, Life With Father , but the Writings and Artwork Series does include letters relating to a publicity stunt in order to promote the book in Chicago. The Writings and Artwork series also represents Day's career as an author and illustrator, including his freelance illustrations for Florence Guy Seabury's The Delicatessen Husband and Lee Wilson Dodd's Pegeen and the Potamus . Some of Day's shorter pieces, such as poetry and short stories, are included in the Printed Material series.

Day's involvement with Yale University, as a student and an alumnus, are traced in his scrapbook dating from 1895 to 1897, correspondence with classmate Albert Galloway Keller, and illustrations for various alumni events from 1909 to 1960.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Correspondence, 1892-1963. II. Writings and Artwork, 1891-1980. III. Personal Papers, 1895-1960. IV. Printed Material, 1924-1936.

did.unittitle: Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series documents the professional and personal life of Clarence Day (including works published posthumously) between 1892 and 1963. Most notably Day's correspondence with the Alfred A. Knopf company provides insight into his career as a writer and illustrator. The series contains both incoming and outgoing correspondence; Day frequently illustrated his correspondence with friends and family, including his letters to Yale University classmate Albert Galloway Keller, Mary Thompson, and Vira Whitehouse.

did.unittitle: Alfred A. Knopf, Inc. to Clarence Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Alfred A. Knopf, Alfred William Koshland, Basil N. Bass, Stimson and a photograph of Clarence and Peg Day

did.unittitle: Alfred A. Knopf, Inc. from Clarence and Peg Day
Adams, J. C., letter from Clarence Day (photostat)
Day, George Parmly, letters from Clarence Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 2 letters and 1 original pencil drawing

did.unittitle: Donaldson, Norman, letters from Clarence Day
Donaldson, Norman, letters concerning Clarence Day
Fuller, Clement Austin, letters from Clarence Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 3 drawings with notes

did.unittitle: Gibney, Virgil Pendleton
Goldenbook Magazine, letter to Clarence Day
Hawley, Margaret Foote, letters from Clarence Day
Hawley, Margaret Foote, letters to Clarence Day
Hawley, Margaret Foote, letters to Geoffrey Parsons
Hawley, Margaret Foote, obituary
Johnson, Alvin, letter to Clarence Day
Johnson, Frederic Blair, letters from Clarence Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 10 letters from Clarence Day and 2 letters from George Parmly Day to Yale University concerning the letters

did.unittitle: Keller, Albert Galloway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains 2 bound volumes of copies of correspondence from Clarence Day to Albert G. Keller

did.unittitle: Keller, Carl T., letters from Clarence Day
Keogh, Andrew
Lewis, Wilmarth Sheldon, letters from Clarence Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 5 letters with envelopes, original pen and ink drawing, and reproduction of a drawing

did.unittitle: MCA Management
Oakley, Louis C., letters from Clarence Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 11 illustrated notes

did.unittitle: Thompson, Mary, letters from Clarence Day
Thompson, Mary, letters from Clarence Day
Welch, Lewis S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 2 letters with drawings from Clarence Day and copies of his replies

did.unittitle: Whitehouse, Vira, from Clarence Day
Whitehouse, Vira, from Clarence Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts of untitled poems, short stories, and a copy of Max

did.unittitle: Various correspondents, including copyright agreement
Writings and Artwork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series documents Clarence Day's career as an author and illustrator, mostly relating to his freelance illustrations for Florence Guy Seabury's The Delicatessen Husband and Lee Wilson Dodd's Pegeen and the Potamus . The series includes Day's work on his book Scenes from the Mesozoic and some early drawings. Some publicity for Life With Father is also in the series, most notably correspondence relating to Alfred A. Knopf's offer of fifty dollars for the best letter explaining why the book did not sell well in Chicago.

did.unittitle: The Delicatessen Husband , original illustrations for Florence Guy Seabury's book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a copy of the printed version

did.unittitle: Third-party letters to Alfred A. Knopf in response to his offer of $50 for the best letter on the reason why Day's Life with Father did not sell well in Chicago
Clipping about Life With Father and typescript memoir (possibly by Alfred A. Knopf) about publishing the book
"Noble Boys," printed version, The Saturday Review
Pegeen and the Potamus , original pen and ink drawing by Clarence Day for Lee Wilson Dodd's book
"To Phoebe," typescript and autograph manuscript of poem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter about the poem from George Parmly Day (1946)

did.unittitle: Scenes from the Mesozoic , typescript, illustrations, original pencil sketches, production file, memos, proofs, and notes
Early pen and ink sketch by Clarence Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter of authentication from George Parmly Day (1947)

did.unittitle: Drawings signed and mounted, 10 pencil drawings and 4 ink drawings
Personal Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains material relating to Clarence Day's affiliation with Yale University as both a student and an alumnus.

did.unittitle: Scrapbook of Yale College and New Haven memorabilia
Class of 1896

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes printed circulars, menus, postal cards, etc. regarding reunion of the Class of 1896, Yale College

did.unittitle: Printed Material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of printed material either by or about Clarence Day. A selection of Day's poetry, short stories, and essays, published between 1924 and 1936 are represented here.

did.unittitle: Clippings of writings by Clarence Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "In Woodlawn" (1926), "Life with Mother" (1936), "Father has a Bad Night" (1933), "Father Thumps on the Floor," "Father Feels Starved," "Father Hires a Cook," "Father and the Crusader's Third Wife," "Father is Firm with his Ailments," "Shorten Kiss," and "Father Lets in the Telephone" (1933)

did.unittitle: Clippings about Clarence Day
Pamphlets
Oversize
Scrapbook of Yale College and New Haven memorabilia
Drawings signed and mounted
